assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 12: '| Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 5 of 5 criteria met |'. Lines 62-70 detail each criterion: (1) Single license filter PASS with validate_license_param and is_in filter, verified by test_list_packages_single_license_filter; (2) Multi-license filter PASS with comma splitting and IN clause, verified by test_list_packages_multi_license_filter; (3) Invalid license 400 PASS with Expression::parse and AppError::BadRequest, verified by test_list_packages_invalid_license_returns_400; (4) Pagination integration PASS with filter applied before count, verified by test_list_packages_license_filter_with_pagination; (5) Response shape unchanged PASS with return type remaining Result<Json<PaginatedResults<PackageSummary>>, AppError>. Additionally, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md each contain detailed PASS verdicts for each criterion.

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 7: '| Scope Containment | PASS | All 3 PR files match task specification exactly (2 modified, 1 created); no out-of-scope or unimplemented files |'. Lines 25-28 provide file-by-file mapping: 'modules/fundamental/src/package/endpoints/list.rs -- modified (listed in Files to Modify)', 'modules/fundamental/src/package/service/mod.rs -- modified (listed in Files to Modify)', 'tests/api/package.rs -- created (listed in Files to Create)'. Line 30: 'No out-of-scope files. No unimplemented files.'

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 5: '| Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments or review body items on the PR |'. No mention of sub-task creation anywhere in the report or criterion files. Line 6: '| Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate |' further confirms no sub-tasks were created.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only analysis/report files: report.md and criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md. The report references code via quotations and analysis (e.g., criterion-1.md quotes code snippets from list.rs and mod.rs) but does not indicate any modifications to source files. All code references are analytical observations, not edit records. The report footer states 'This comment was AI-generated by sdlc-workflow/verify-pr' confirming it is a read-only verification comment.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md concludes with '### Overall: PASS' and 'All checks passed. The PR correctly implements the license filter feature...' but contains no merge action, no gh pr merge command, and no indication of auto-merge. The report presents findings in a structured table format with detailed sub-sections for human review. The footer 'This comment was AI-generated by sdlc-workflow/verify-pr' indicates it is a review comment, not an automated merge action.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 10: '| Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No secrets, credentials, API keys, or sensitive patterns detected in added lines across 3 files |'. Lines 44-51 detail the scan: 'Scanned all added lines across 3 files. No matches detected for any sensitive pattern category: No hardcoded passwords or secrets, No API keys or tokens, No private keys or certificates, No .env files or dotenv-style secrets, No cloud provider credentials, No database credentials or connection strings with embedded passwords'.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | 1 new test file (tests/api/package.rs) with 4 test functions; no modified or deleted test files |'. Lines 104-106 expand: 'tests/api/package.rs is a new file (listed in Files to Create). It adds 4 test functions with 80 lines of test code. No existing test files were modified or deleted. Classification is purely additive.'

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality (combining Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality) and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: report.md contains four domain sections: (1) Intent Alignment (line 21) with Scope Containment PASS (line 23), Diff Size PASS (line 31), Commit Traceability PASS (line 37); (2) Security (line 42) with Sensitive Pattern Scan PASS (line 44); (3) Correctness (line 55) with CI Status PASS (line 57), Acceptance Criteria PASS 5/5 (line 60), Verification Commands N/A (line 72); (4) Style/Conventions (line 77) with Repetitive Test Detection PASS (line 82), Test Documentation PASS (line 92), Eval Quality N/A (line 100), Test Change Classification ADDITIVE (line 104). The summary table (lines 7-15) aggregates: Test Quality PASS combines Repetitive Test Detection PASS, Test Documentation PASS, and Eval Quality N/A.

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: Intent Alignment (lines 25-28): file-by-file scope comparison listing each file with modification status and task specification match. Diff Size (lines 33-34): '~105 additions, ~2 deletions across 3 files' with expected vs actual file count. Security (lines 44-51): six specific pattern categories scanned with individual results. Correctness (lines 62-70): per-criterion code-level verification citing specific functions (validate_license_param, Expression::parse, is_in), error types (AppError::BadRequest), and test names. Style/Conventions (lines 84-90): individual analysis of all 4 test functions with assertion comparisons and parameterization assessment; lines 93-98: specific doc comment text quoted for each test function.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13: 'Test Quality | PASS | Repetitive Test Detection: PASS (...); Test Documentation: PASS (...); Eval Quality: N/A'. Lines 100-102: '**Eval Quality -- N/A** No eval result reviews found on the PR. No eval quality assessment performed.' Test Quality is PASS combining only Repetitive Test Detection and Test Documentation, with Eval Quality not affecting the combination due to N/A status.

assertion_results.text: No eval failure sub-tasks are created because Eval Quality is N/A — eval failure sub-tasks are only created when Eval Quality is WARN (at least one eval assertion failed)
assertion_results.evidence: Eval Quality is N/A (report.md lines 100-102: 'No eval result reviews found on the PR. No eval quality assessment performed.'). No mention of eval failure sub-tasks anywhere in report.md or any criterion file. The only sub-task reference is in Root-Cause Investigation (line 6): 'No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate', confirming no sub-tasks of any kind were created.

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 5-6: '| Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments or review body items on the PR |' and '| Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate |'. These two verdicts appear in the top-level summary table (lines 4-15) but do NOT appear under any of the four domain sections (Intent Alignment lines 21-38, Security lines 42-52, Correctness lines 55-74, Style/Conventions lines 77-106), confirming they are determined independently by the orchestrator rather than by domain sub-agents.
